name: Rendering performance (React/Next.js)
description: MEDIUM: reduce paint/layout/hydration issues. Includes: content-visibility, animate SVG wrapper, hoist static JSX, SVG precision, hydration mismatch patterns, conditional rendering gotchas, script loading strategy, resource hints.

metadata.intent: Improve rendering performance and avoid hydration issues in React + Next.js

must_use: Prefer CSS and browser primitives over JS work where possible
must_not: Hide real hydration bugs by suppressing warnings broadly
conventions: Use targeted fixes: suspense boundaries, stable markup, expected mismatch suppression only when intentional.

steps.description: Animate a wrapper element (div) instead of the SVG element for better GPU acceleration support.
Use `content-visibility: auto` with `contain-intrinsic-size` for long lists to skip offscreen layout/paint.
Hoist static JSX (large SVG nodes, skeletons) outside components to avoid recreating elements each render.
Reduce SVG coordinate precision (SVGO) to shrink assets without visible degradation.
For client-only values (theme/localStorage), avoid SSR breakage + flicker using pre-hydration DOM initialization (targeted inline script).
Use `suppressHydrationWarning` only for known intentional mismatches (dates/locale), not to hide bugs.
Avoid render-blocking scripts; use Next.js `next/script` strategies or `defer/async` appropriately.
Avoid `count && <Badge/>` when count can be 0; prefer explicit ternary to prevent rendering `0`.
Use React DOM resource hints (prefetchDNS/preconnect/preload/preinit) for critical domains/fonts/modules.
